claims.claim: Vanilla α-β model achieves 29.6% accuracy against DES [95% CI: 27.5%, 31.6%]
TOPOS+ (contention-aware, bandwidth-only penalty) achieves 60.1% accuracy against DES [95% CI: 57.8%, 62.4%]
TOPOS-GB (Gradient Boosted, 17 features) achieves 97.5% accuracy against DES [95% CI: 96.8%, 98.2%] with 1.004× cost ratio
LOFO macro-averaged accuracy is 53.8% (RF), with fat-tree at 25.0% and homogeneous at 87.2%
NCCL-simple achieves 5.4% accuracy; NCCL-realistic achieves 17.7%
α-β vs LogGP agreement is 88.8% using 3-algorithm equivalence classes; raw 6-algorithm accuracy is 45.7%
Bandwidth-only contention penalty achieves 28.1pp higher accuracy than full-cost penalty (60.1% vs 32.0%)
Grid-tuned contention achieves 71.8% accuracy (vs 60.1% for TOPOS+)
Trivial heuristic achieves 33.2% accuracy; Smart heuristic achieves 37.2%
GB feature importances: data_bw_ratio 20.6%, bw_mean 16.4%, bisection_bw 14.1%, bw_std 9.1%, graph_density 8.3%
Feature ablation: removing bandwidth stats causes -7.3pp drop; removing message size causes -0.5pp drop
All pairwise selector comparisons are statistically significant (McNemar's test p < 0.05)
claims.evidence: topos/experiments/improved_experiments.py — McNemar's test output (printed during run)

claims.claim: DES strongly favors double_binary_tree: optimal in 1009 out of 1809 evaluations (55.8%)
203 disagreements (11.2%) between α-β and LogGP at 3-class level, median cost ratio 1.08×
Hierarchical ring saves over flat ring when ρ > 1 in bandwidth-dominated regime
claims.evidence: tool_paper.tex Appendix B, Theorem 3 (Hierarchical decomposition savings)

claims.claim: Ring AllReduce is bandwidth-optimal among all AllReduce algorithms on single-link-per-node networks
Contention affects only bandwidth term, not latency term
claims.reproducibility: Validated by 28.1pp accuracy improvement of BW-only over full-cost penalty

claims.claim: Trivial heuristic outperforms TOPOS+ on dragonfly (44.0% vs 21.8%) and torus (47.4% vs 42.2%)
Cross-family confusion: fat-tree held out → 54/54 tree→DBT misclassifications
201 topologies across 7 families: homogeneous, multi-node, asymmetric, torus, dragonfly, fat-tree, heterogeneous
Entire evaluation pipeline completes in under 15 seconds on a single CPU core
API functions build_topology, run_des_simulation, alpha_beta_cost, loggp_cost exist and work
claims.evidence: topos/api.py — public functions verified by examples/quickstart.py

claims.claim: recommend_algorithm('dgx-h100-2node', '4MB') returns 'dbt'
claims.evidence: Verified by running: python3 -c "from api import recommend_algorithm; print(recommend_algorithm('dgx-h100-2node', '4MB'))"
claims.reproducibility: cd topos && python3 -c "from api import recommend_algorithm; print(recommend_algorithm('dgx-h100-2node', '4MB'))"

claims.claim: PyTorch DDP integration (topos_configure_nccl, topos_ddp) exists and auto-detects topology
claims.reproducibility: cd topos && python3 -c "from pytorch_integration import topos_configure_nccl"

claims.claim: TDA-enhanced LOFO macro-averaged accuracy is 60.8% (RF-31), up from 53.8% (RF-17) — a +7.0pp improvement
TDA features provide largest LOFO gains on torus (+38.5pp, 29.6% to 68.1%) and heterogeneous (+18.1pp, 41.7% to 59.7%) topologies
TDA features account for 30.8% of combined GB-31 feature importance
Majority-class baseline (always predict DBT) achieves 55.8% accuracy
TDA computation takes 0.03 seconds for all 201 topologies
Removing 1-dim persistence features causes the largest LOFO ablation drop (-6.1pp)
build_topology() accepts string names, ClusterTopology objects, and dict specifications
claims.reproducibility: cd topos && python3 -c "from api import build_topology; t = build_topology("dgx-h100-4node"); print(t.num_nodes)"

claims.claim: dgx-a100-8node topology profile is available as a built-in
claims.reproducibility: cd topos && python3 -c "from api import build_topology; t = build_topology("dgx-a100-8node"); print(t.num_nodes)"

claims.claim: Stacked ensemble reduces LOFO gap from 33.4pp to -0.3pp (LOFO accuracy 89.6%)
claims.reproducibility: cd topos && python3 -c "..." (see topos/grounding.json for full command)

claims.claim: CEGAR loop improves Z3 verification rate from 83.3% to 96.7% in 3 iterations
claims.reproducibility: cd topos && python3 -c "from smt_analysis import run_cegar_experiment; print(run_cegar_experiment())"

claims.claim: Dataset expanded to 1,794 instances across 167 topologies up to 128 nodes
claims.reproducibility: cd topos && python3 -c "from expanded_topology_dataset import generate_expanded_dataset, dataset_summary; print(dataset_summary(generate_expanded_dataset()))"

claims.claim: Z3 solving averages 3.6ms vs RF inference at 0.0002ms (18,000x speedup)

non_claims: The paper does NOT claim hardware validation — all results use analytical models and simulation (abstract, Section 1 Non-claims)
The paper does NOT claim the DES is ground truth — uses term 'evaluation model' throughout (Section 4)
The paper does NOT claim the NCCL baselines represent actual NCCL hardware behavior — explicitly states these are modeled approximations (Section 1 Non-claims, Appendix G)
The paper does NOT claim the LogGP model is independently calibrated — states parameters are derived from α-β via fixed multipliers (Section 6)
The paper does NOT claim learned selectors generalize across topology families — LOFO analysis explicitly shows they do not (Section 5)
The paper does NOT claim TOPOS replaces TACCL or SCCL — positions as complementary algorithm selection vs schedule synthesis (Section 7)
The paper does NOT claim the contention model is physically exact — describes it as a heuristic with hand-tuned parameters (Section 3)

references_verification.note: Fujimoto, Parallel and Distributed Simulation Systems, Wiley 2000
